article_meta.title: A copy of the Ten Commandments is posted along with other historical documents in a hallway of the Georgia Capitol on June 20, 2024, in Atlanta

reasoning: No attribution violations, but several overreaching flags on largely neutral author prose and at least one mismatched bias type. The piece reads as straightforward AP reporting; the model inferred slant where context is typical, and misapplied 'Elite vs. Populist Bias.'

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged text is from the author’s prose; no quoted or clearly attributed source language was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged items are standard context (party attribution, nominating president) or hinge on an incomplete sentence likely due to copy error, not clear author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The article contains little to no clear author-level bias; nothing significant was missed.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labeling the judge’s nominator as 'Elite vs. Populist Bias' is incorrect; the slant labels are debatable at best.
